feat: redesign onboarding with Echo intro-first flow

Restructure 3-step onboarding so users understand what Echo is
before being asked for their name:

  Introduces the core concept (echo/mirror metaphor) with animation
  Language toggle remains on first screen

Step 1 — Name: "Now, let Echo know you."
  User gives their name after understanding the product
  Warmer framing vs cold form

Step 2 — How it works: "Write. Echo listens. Then ask."
  Concrete examples of what to ask Echo ("How have I been feeling?")

Updates translations in both EN and ES.

commit.message:
Fix scanner upload: CSY missing PROTECT param and malformed SIN/GIN/CIN commands

Root cause of "Scanner returned invalid system index" on every system:
- CSY command requires CSY,[SYS_TYPE],[PROTECT] but we sent CSY,[SYS_TYPE]
  with no PROTECT field; scanner returns -1 (no resource) for malformed commands.
  Fixed: send PROTECT=0 (unprotected).

Also fix malformed command formats discovered via protocol spec (PDF):
- SIN: fields after DLY were wrong (data_skip in RSV slot, "AUTO"/"8" in
  wrong positions). Fixed to use correct 12 empty placeholder fields then
  NUMBER_TAG/AGC_ANALOG/AGC_DIGITAL/P25WAITING.
- GIN: was missing trailing LATITUDE,LONGITUDE,RANGE,GPS_ENABLE fields.
  Fixed: append ,,,, placeholders.
- CIN: was missing RECORD,AUDIO_TYPE,P25NAC,NUMBER_TAG,ALT_COLOR,
  ALT_PATTERN,VOL_OFFSET. Fixed: append correct trailing fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: move admin pages into (protected) route group to stop redirect loop

The login page was inside the same layout that checked auth, causing an
infinite redirect for unauthenticated users. Fix:
- Move all protected admin pages into app/admin/(protected)/ so the
  auth-checking layout only wraps those routes
- app/admin/login/page.tsx stays outside (protected) — no auth gate
- Simplify (protected)/layout.tsx: always redirects to /admin/login
- Update middleware: admin subdomain / → redirect to /login, authenticated
  /login → redirect to /dashboard; rewrite all other paths to /admin/*

feat: add software engineering handbook

Complete workflow system for managing 25+ repositories with AI agents.

Documents:
- Project initialization (templates, structure)
- Feature development workflow (issue → release)
- Issue management (templates, labels, lifecycle)
- Pull request process (template, checklist, merge)
- Code review guidelines (checklist, feedback)
- Release process (semantic versioning, deployment)
- Documentation standards (README, code docs)
- AI agent integration (project context, prompts)
- Repository templates

Principle: Human-driven, AI-executed - humans decide WHAT,
AI does HOW. Consistency across all repositories through
standardized templates and rules.

commit.message:
fix: create_callback, suggest_availability, AI fabrication (3 blockers)

1. elevenlabs-create-callback: status 'pending' → 'new' (violated DB CHECK
   constraint causing 100% failure rate). Handle null customerId gracefully
   (text simulator has no caller phone — log to session, skip opportunity).

2. text-conversation: add suggest_availability tool + endpoint mapping.
   AI was calling it but it wasn't in the tool list — returned Unknown.

3. agentBasePrompts SERVICE_AGENT: strengthen anti-fabrication PRIME DIRECTIVE 7.
   Added explicit rules: no invented promotions/seasonal specials, no maintenance
   plans/memberships not in data, PRESSURE RULE — when caller pushes back on
   price, use create_callback instead of inventing discounts.

commit.message:
fix: replace Host-header-based fetchScans with getScans from lib/api.ts (#83)

Removes the inline fetchScans() function that read the attacker-controlled
Host header to construct the API URL (SSRF risk). Uses the existing getScans()
from lib/api.ts which derives the base URL from VERCEL_URL env var or localhost.

Closes #82

commit.message:
feat: add auto-approval rules with inline manage UI (#41)

* feat: add auto-approval rules system with inline manage UI

Add a rules engine that can auto-approve/deny Claude tool calls via
hook events. Rules can be added manually (am rule add) or learned from
worktree settings (am rule sync). The settings panel replaces the old
diff view with an inline "Manage Rules" sub-view for browsing and
removing rules without leaving the TUI.

- Add Rule type, rules storage (rules.json), and CRUD operations
- Add rule CLI commands: list, add, remove, sync, clear, apply, restore
- Evaluate rules in hook-event PreToolUse for auto-approve/deny
- Add applyGlobalRules toggle to persist rules to ~/.claude/settings.json
- Replace diffRules field with manageRules inline sub-view in Settings
- Auto-sync learned rules from worktrees when feature enabled
- Track am-managed permissions separately for clean add/remove

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add missing path mocks and rules mock for tests

Add RULES_PATH and AM_MANAGED_PERMISSIONS_PATH to the test setup
paths mock, and add rules.js mock to SettingsPanel tests.

repository.description: Build Digital FTEs that work 24/7. Your domain expertise—whether in sales, legal, finance, or healthcare—becomes an autonomous AI agent that never sleeps.

commit.message:
enforce: strengthen POST-EMIT-VALIDATION as non-negotiable blocking gate

- Added explicit section on POST-EMIT-VALIDATION requiring witnessed execution
- Reframed all validation language as mandatory blocking gates (CANNOT SKIP)
- Emphasized immediate execution after EMIT (no deferral, no assumption)
- Added requirement to load and test actual modified files from disk
- Changed from negative list (what not to do) to positive enforcement (what must happen)
- Documented strong completion evidence patterns with real execution examples
- Rewritten VALIDATION LAYER 2 as absolute requirement with clear consequences
- Updated state transition rules to block progression without proof

POST-EMIT-VALIDATION is now enforced as:
- BLOCKING GATE (cannot proceed to VERIFY without it)
- IMMEDIATE (must execute after EMIT, next action)
- WITNESSED (actual output must be documented)
- ON DISK (actual modified files must be loaded and executed)
- NON-DEFERRABLE (no 'later', no 'assume', no skipping)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: allow null date in getSectors() return type (#84)

The /api/v1/sectors endpoint returns { date: null, sectors: [] } when
the sector_momentum table is empty. Aligns the client type with the
actual API contract to prevent silent runtime errors.

Closes #81

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add Ch 32 Lesson 4 — Reading a Test (def and assert vocabulary)

Bridge lesson introducing two vocabulary words (def, assert) so learners
can read test code before writing their first test in Chapter 33.
